title: 第 37 節
en: 1 It does so in the eye, and likewise with the person in the eye, who is not affected by anything. Cf. Kh. Up. IV, 14, 3.
The commentator takes light, day, &c. as persons, or devatas. Cf. Kh. Up. V, 10, 1.

3 If any mistakes happen during the performance of a sacrifice, as described before, they are remedied by certain interjectional IV PRAPArffAKA, 1 6 KHANDA, 5. 69 Because moving along he purifies everything, therefore he is the sacrifice. Of that sacrifice there are two ways, by mind and by speech.

2. The Brahman priest performs one of them in his mind l, the Hotrt, Adhvaryu, and Udgatrz priests perform the other by words. When the Brahman priest, after the Prataranuvaka ceremony has begun, but before the recitation of the Paridhaniya hymn, has (to break his silence and) to speak, 3. He performs perfectly the one way only (that by words), but the other is injured. As a man walking on one foot, or a carriage going on one wheel, is injured, his sacrifice is injured, and with the injured sacrifice the sacrificer is injured; yes, having sacrificed, he becomes worse.

4. But when after the Prataranuvaka ceremony has begun, and before the recitation of the Paridha niya hymn, the Brahman priest has not (to break his silence and) to speak, they perform both ways perfectly, and neither of them is injured.

5. As a man walking on two legs and a carriage going on two wheels gets on, so his sacrifice gets on, and with the successful sacrifice the sacrificer gets on ; yes, having sacrificed, he becomes better.
syllables (vyah/Yti), the nature of which is next described. All this is supposed to take place in the forest.

1 While the other priests perform the sacrifice, the Brahman priest has to remain silent, following the whole sacrifice in his mind, and watching that no mistake be committed. If a mistake is committed, he has to correct it, and for that purpose certain corrective penances (praya^itta) are enjoined. The performance of the Brahman priest resembles the meditations of the sages in the forest, and therefore this chapter is here inserted.
70 JTffANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.
Seventeenth Khaa^a.
i. Pra^apati brooded over the worlds, and from them thus brooded on he squeezed out the essences, Agni (fire) from the earth, Vayu (air) from the sky, Aditya (the sun) from heaven.
2. He brooded over these three deities, and from them thus brooded on he squeezed out the essences, the Rik verses from Agni, the Ya^us verses from Vayu, the Saman verses from Aditya.

3. He brooded over the threefold knowledge (the three Vedas), and from it thus brooded on he squeezed out the essences, the sacred interjection Bhus from the Rik verses, the sacred interjection Bhuvas from the Ya^us verses, the sacred interjection Svar from the Saman verses.

4. If the sacrifice is injured from the Rig-veda side, let him offer a libation in the Garhapatya fire, saying, BhM, Svaha ! Thus does he bind together and heal, by means of the essence and the power of the Rik verses themselves, whatever break the Rik sacrifice may have suffered.

5. If the sacrifice is injured from the Ya^ur-veda side, let him offer a libation in the Dakshiwa fire, saying, Bhuva/;, Svaha ! Thus does he bind together and heal, by means of the essence and the power of the Ya^us verses themselves, whatever break the Ya^us sacrifice may have suffered.

6. If the sacrifice is injured by the Sama-veda side, let him offer a libation in the Ahavaniya fire, saying, Sva./i, Svaha ! Thus does he bind together and heal, by means of the essence and the power of the Saman verses themselves, whatever break the Saman sacrifice may have suffered.

IV PRAPA7WAKA, I 7 KHANDA, 9. 7 I 7. As one binds (softens) gold by means of lava/^a * (borax), and silver by means of gold, and tin by means of silver, and lead by means of tin, and iron (loha) by means of lead, and wood by means of iron, or also by means of leather, 8. Thus does one bind together and heal any break in the sacrifice by means of (the Vyahmis or sacrificial interjections which are) the essence and strength of the three worlds, of the deities, and of the threefold knowledge. That sacrifice is healed 2 in which there is a Brahman priest who knows this.

9. That sacrifice is inclined towards the north (in the right way) in which there is a Brahman priest who knows this. And with regard to such a Brahman priest there is the following Gatha3: ' Whereever it falls back, thither the man 4 goes,' — viz. the Brahman only, as one of the Ritv'xg priests. ' He saves the Kurus as a mare ' (viz. a Brahman priest who 1 Lavaraa, a kind of salt, explained by kshara and /anka or /ankana. It is evidently borax, which is still imported from the East Indies under the name of tincal, and used as a flux in chemical processes.

2 Bhesha^akr/ta, explained by bhesha^-ena 'iva kritak sawskrzta^, and also by ^ikitsakena surikshitena ' esha yag-no bhavati,' which looks as if the commentator had taken it as a genitive of bhesha^akrz't.

3 This Gatha (or, according to »Sahkara, Anugatha) is probably a Gayatri, though Anandagiri says that it is not in the Gayatri or any other definite metre. It may have been originally ' yato yata avartate, tattad ga£X7zati manava/z, kurun ajvabhirakshati.' This might be taken from an old epic ballad, ' Wherever the army fell back, thither the man went ; the mare (mares being preferred to stallions in war) saves the Kurus.' That verse was applied to the Brahman priest succouring the sacrifice, whenever it seemed to waver, and protecting the Kurus, i.e. the performers of the sacrifice.
4 Manava, explained from mauna, or manana, but possibly originally, a descendant of Manu.
72 A'tfANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.
knows this, saves the sacrifice, the sacrifices and all the other priests). Therefore let a man make him who knows this his Brahman priest, not one who does not know it, who does not know it.
First Khaa^da.
i. He who knows the oldest and the best becomes himself the oldest and the best. Breath indeed is the oldest and the best.
2. He who knows the richest, becomes himself the richest. Speech indeed is the richest.
3. He who knows the firm rest, becomes himself firm in this world and in the next. The eye indeed is the firm rest.
4. He who knows success, his wishes succeed, both his divine and human wishes. The ear indeed is success.
5. He who knows the home, becomes a home of his people. The mind indeed is the home.
6. The five senses quarrelled together 2, who was the best, saying, I am better, I am better.
zh: 它在眼睛中這樣做，同樣也在眼睛中的人身上如此，而此人不受任何事物的影響。參見《香德奧義書》(Kh. Up.) IV, 14, 3。
評註者將光、白晝等視為人格化的存在，或提婆。參見《歌者奧義書》（Kh. Up.）V, 10, 1。
如果在之前所述的祭祀儀式表演中發生任何過失，這些過失便可透過某些感嘆詞加以補救。因為他行進時淨化一切，因此他就是祭祀。那祭祀有兩種方式：心念之方式與言語之方式。

2. 婆羅門祭司在心中進行其中一種¹，而侯特祭司、阿陀婆耶祭司和烏德蓋特祭司則以言語進行另一種。當婆羅門祭司在普拉塔蘭烏瓦卡儀式已開始之後、但在帕里陀哈尼亞讚歌的誦唱之前，不得不（打破沉默）說話時³，他只能完美地進行其中一種方式（即以言語進行的方式），但另一種方式則受到損傷。就如同一個人只用一隻腳行走，或一輛馬車只用一個輪子行駛會受到損傷一樣，他的祭祀受到了損傷，而祭祀者會因這個受損的祭祀而受到損傷；是的，在獻祭之後，他變得更加墮落。
4. 但當普拉塔拉努瓦卡（Prataranuvaka）儀式已經開始，而帕里達尼亞讚歌（Paridha niya）的吟誦尚未進行之際，婆羅門祭司無須（打破沉默而）說話，他們便能以兩種方式完美地執行儀式，而兩者皆不受損傷。
5. 正如一個人靠雙腿行走、一輛馬車靠雙輪前進一樣，他的祭祀也得以進行，隨著祭祀的成功，祭祀者也得以進行；是的，經過祭祀之後，他變得更好了。
音節（vyahṛti），其性質接下來被描述。所有這一切都被認為是在森林中進行的。
當其他祭司執行祭祀時，婆羅門祭司必須保持沉默，在心中跟隨整個祭祀過程，並留意是否有任何錯誤發生。若有錯誤發生，他必須加以糾正，為此目的特定的補贖苦行（praya^itta）乃被規定。婆羅門祭司的執行方式與林中聖賢的冥想相似，因此本章節在此被插入。
70 頻陀奧義書（JAIANDOGYA-UPANISHAD）。
第十七個卡阿（Khaa）
梵天（Prajāpati）沈思默想於諸世界，由此沈思默想而從中擠出精粹：阿耆尼（Agni，火）出自地界，伐由（Vayu，風）出自天空，阿迪提耶（Aditya，太陽）出自天界。
2. 他思索著這三位提婆，並從他這樣思索的這三位提婆身上，他擠出了精華──從阿格尼（Agni）中擠出梨俱經文，從伐由（Vayu）中擠出夜柔經文，從阿迪提耶（Aditya）中擠出娑摩經文。
他沉思於三重知識（三部吠陀），自此沉思而得，他從中擠取精華，從梨克詩句中擠出神聖的感嘆詞「薄呼」（Bhus），從亞祀詩句中擠出神聖的感嘆詞「薄伐」（Bhuvas），從薩摩詩句中擠出神聖的感嘆詞「斯瓦」（Svar）。
4. 倘若梨俱吠陀祭祀有所損傷，則應於迦羅波帝耶火祭中獻奠，誦念：「BhM，薩婆訶！」(Bhūr Bhuvaḥ Svaḥ, Svaha!)由此，他藉由梨俱詩句本身的本質與力量，將梨俱吠陀祭祀所蒙受的任何破損結合修復。
5. 若祭祀因雅祖吠陀（Yajur-veda）一方而受損，則應令其在南火（Dakshiwa fire）中奉獻奠祭，誦言「婆訶！蘇婆訶！」（Bhuva/，Svaha!）由此，他藉由雅祙經句（Ya^us verses）本身的精質與力量，綰合並復原雅祙祭祀可能遭受的任何破損。
6. 倘若薩摩吠陀（Sama-veda）方面對祭祀造成傷害，則當於阿哈瓦尼亞祭火（Ahavaniya fire）中獻祭酒，吟誦「斯瓦哈！斯瓦哈！」（Sva./i, Svaha!）。如此，他便藉由薩摩讚歌（Saman verses）本身的本質與力量，將薩摩祭祀所遭受的任何破損結合在一起並予以治癒。
IV 普拉帕特卡，I 7 坤達，9. 7 I 7. 如同人用硇砂（lavana）柔和黃金，用黃金柔和白銀，用白銀柔和錫，用錫柔和鉛，用鉛柔和鐵（loha），用鐵或同樣用皮革柔和木料，8. 因此人以（毗亞赫米斯或祭祀間嘆詞，其為）三界、諸神靈及三重知識的本質與力量，將祭祀中任何的裂隙綁縛在一起並治癒之。若其中有一位懂得此理的婆羅門祭司，則該祭祀得以治癒。

9. 凡有懂得此理的婆羅門祭司之犧牲，乃是朝向北方（以正確的方式）傾斜的。關於這樣的婆羅門祭司，有如下的頌歌：「無論它倒向何處，人便往那裡去」——即婆羅門祭司，作為梨俱吠陀祭司之一。「如同母馬一樣拯救庫魯人」（即一位婆羅門祭司，他 1 拉瓦納（Lavaraa）是一種鹽類，由克沙拉（kshara）和藍卡（/anka）或藍卡納（/ankana）來說明。它顯然就是硼砂，至今仍以「天然鹼」（tincal）之名從東印度進口，並用作化學過程中的熔劑。
2 Bhesha^akr/ta，由 bhesha^-ena 'iva kritak sawskrzta^ 解釋，亦由 ^ikitsakena surikshitena ' esha yag-no bhavati' 解釋，看起來評註者是把它當作 bhesha^akrz't 的屬格。

這首讚歌（或根據商羯羅的說法，是附讚歌〔Anugatha〕）可能是一首迦耶特里讚（Gayatri），儘管阿難陀吉里說它不符合迦耶特里或任何其他確定的韻律。它最初可能是這樣的：「yato yata avartate, tattad gacchati manavah, kurun ajvabhirakshati.」這句詩可能取自一首古老的史詩歌謠：「軍隊往何處撤退，勇士就往何處去；母馬（戰爭中更受偏好勝過公馬）拯救了俱盧族。」這句詩被應用於婆羅門祭司身上，祭祀每當似乎將要動搖之際，他就出現來扶持，並保護俱盧族，亦即執行祭祀的人。
馬納瓦（Manava），由摩納（mauna）或曼那那（manana）解釋而來，但其原意可能是馬奴（Manu）的後裔。
72 愛坦陀盧迦奧義書（Aitareya-Upanishad）。
懂得此理者，救贖了祭祀、諸祭祀及其他一切祭司）。因此，讓人以懂得此理者為其婆羅門祭司，而非不懂者、不懂此理者。
第一課。
他誰知道最古老和最高尚的事物，誰自己就會成為最古老和最高尚的。氣息（prāṇa）確實是最古老和最高尚的。
2. 知曉最豐富者，自身亦成為最豐富者。言辭誠然是最豐富的。
3. 誰知曉堅固之支點，他自己在此世界和來世都將變得堅固。眼睛確實就是堅固之支點。
4. 誰知曉成功，他的願望便得以實現，其神聖的願望與人間的願望皆然。耳朵確實就是成功。
5. 認識家的人，就成為自己人民的家。心靈確實就是家。
6. 五種感官相互爭論誰最優越，各自說道：我最好，我最好。
